description: Unique identifier of the ad responsible for generating the conversion or event. Joins to ad_history.id.
Timestamp of the last modification to the ad associated with this conversion.
Internal numerical index used for ordering or referencing conversion records.
Information about the mobile or web application tied to this conversion (if applicable).
Unique identifier of the specific conversion event (e.g., purchase ID or form submission ID).
Metadata describing the creative assets (text, media, layout) associated with the ad.
Indicates the dataset or data source where this conversion record originated.
Name of the event tracked, such as Purchase, Lead, or AddToCart.
Category of event (e.g., onsite engagement, offsite conversion, or lead submission).
Facebook Pixel identifier that captured this event on a website.
Specific event name tracked by the Facebook Pixel (e.g., PageView, AddToCart).
Identifier or reference to a Lead Generation form used for collecting customer data.
The ad object associated with the event, such as a Page Post or Offer.
Data about any offer or promotion related to the ad campaign.
Identifier for older Facebook offsite tracking pixels used before Facebook Pixel adoption.
Facebook Page linked to this ad or event.
Reference to the specific Facebook Post associated with the event.
Refers to a Question or Poll post used in interactive campaigns.
The response or answer submitted by a user (e.g., in lead forms or surveys).
Subcategory of the event (e.g., video_view, page_like, or lead_submission).
Defines the action taken by the user (e.g., link_click, purchase, form_submission).
Identifier of the user, system, or app that created the event.
The domain where the conversion occurred (e.g., example.com).
Creator or owner of the offer related to the ad.
Parent Page or entity associated with the Page for hierarchical tracking.
Wall or timeline where the post appeared (user wall, Page wall, etc.).
Specifies the wall or feed location of the post in context.
The object associated with the post (e.g., image, video, shared link).
The account or page that created the Question post.
Timestamp when Fivetran last synced this row. Internal metadata, never expose to users.
